_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Statewide-exec batch: OH Gov DeWine, OH LtGov Tressel, OH Auditor Faber, OH AG Yost, OK AG Drummond. Evidence-first, every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; currency fixes (Yost resigned to ADF 2026-06-07; Drummond in 2026 OK gov runoff; Tressel/DeWine not running 2026; Faber running for OH AG).

mike-dewine.notes_set: Two-term Governor of Ohio (since 2019), term-limited and NOT on the 2026 ballot (confirmed still in office June 2026). Institutionalist Republican with a mixed record. STRONG: signed the 2019 Heartbeat ban (SB 23); opposed 2023 Issue 1; signed universal EdChoice vouchers (HB 33), the Parents' Bill of Rights (HB 8), porn age-verification (HB 96), the SB 104 bathroom bill, strict photo voter ID (HB 458), the Zuckerbucks ban, constitutional carry (SB 215, 2022) and stand-your-ground (SB 175, 2021). DEVIATIONS (all documented): VETOED HB 68 (gender-care/trans-sports ban — legislature overrode Jan 2024); called for ABOLISHING Ohio's death penalty (June 2026, reversing lifelong support, 7-yr execution moratorium); kept ACA Medicaid expansion (reversed prior opposition); pushed a red-flag/expanded-background-check plan after the 2019 Dayton shooting; vetoed a 2025 budget item to protect youth-shelter pronoun affirmation and cut $35.1M from non-chartered religious schools; defended legally-present Springfield Haitians and rebuked Trump's 'eating pets' attacks. Confidence evidence_state.

1.note: Signed the 6-week Heartbeat restriction, not abolition; in the 2023 Issue 1 fight promised to RELAX the ban toward a majority-acceptable compromise — restriction posture, not abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by SBA Pro-Life and Ohio Right to Life; no abortion-industry PAC funding found (SOFT).

0.note: Voted for one-man-one-woman marriage definition as US Senator; defended Ohio's marriage ban as AG.

1.note: As AG urged SCOTUS to uphold Ohio's same-sex-marriage ban in Obergefell (HARD).

4.note: Signed HB 8 Parents' Bill of Rights banning gender-ideology/sexuality content K-3 + parental notification (HARD). NOTE: vetoed HB 68 trans-care/sports ban — see notes; cell [2] held null for that mixed record.

0.note: Signed HB 33 making EdChoice vouchers universal to every Ohio student (HARD).

1.note: HB 8 mandates parental notification on sexuality/health-care content in schools (HARD). Counterweight: vetoed HB 68 minor gender-care ban — noted.

2.note: HB 8 limits gender-ideology instruction in lower grades (SOGI) (HARD).

3.note: Signed HB 96 requiring government-ID age verification for adult sites (HARD).

2.note: 2025 budget veto PROTECTED youth shelters that affirm social gender transition (preferred pronouns) — contrary to opposing compelled/pronoun speech (HARD).

3.note: HB 8 mandates a religious release-time policy in all schools (HARD).

1.note: Signed HB 458 strict photo voter ID (HARD); citizenship-verification component not separately documented.

3.note: HB 458 limited counties to one drop box and tightened absentee deadlines (HARD).

4.note: Signed Ohio's ban on private ('Zuckerbucks') election funding in the HB 110 budget (HARD).

0.note: Deployed Ohio State Highway Patrol + $2.5M to Springfield for public safety; career former prosecutor/AG (HARD).

4.note: June 2026: publicly called to ABOLISH Ohio's death penalty, reversing decades of support; imposed a 7-yr execution moratorium — contrary to the magistrate bearing the sword (HARD).

1.note: Reversed his ACA opposition and committed to KEEPING federally-funded Medicaid expansion — embraced federal strings rather than declining them (HARD).
Proposed a red-flag-style 'STRONG Ohio' plan + expanded background checks after the 2019 Dayton shooting (HARD).

jim-tressel.notes_set: Lt. Governor of Ohio, appointed by Gov. DeWine Feb 2025 (former Ohio State football coach / Youngstown State president). CURRENCY: considered a 2026 governor run but DECLINED Sept 19, 2025; NOT a 2026 candidate, leaving office with DeWine Jan 2027. Almost no policy/voting record — most rubric cells are honestly NULL. The one well-documented strength is a decades-long public Christian witness (FCA/Athletes in Action, called coaching 'my ministry,' authored a Christian book). The one clear DEVIATION: on abortion he deferred to Ohio's 2023 voter-passed abortion-rights amendment — 'when it's the law, you support it' — i.e., not a pro-life/abolition stance. Confidence evidence_state (scored on his own cited statements; the rest null, no record).

1.note: On confirmation (Feb 2025), deferred to Ohio's voter-passed abortion-rights amendment: 'When it's the law, you support it' — affirms the pro-choice status quo, not abolition (HARD).

0.note: Decades of openly professing Christ in public roles (FCA, 'my ministry,' Christian-themed book) — models/affirms the right to profess Christ in all spheres including public office (HARD).

set.office: State Auditor (sitting; 2026 candidate for Ohio Attorney General)

keith-faber.notes_set: Ohio Auditor of State (since 2019), term-limited; RUNNING FOR OHIO ATTORNEY GENERAL in 2026 (first declared GOP candidate Jan 2025; endorsed by Club for Growth and Ohio Right to Life PAC). Long prior record: Ohio House, Ohio Senate (President 2013-2016). Consistently conservative, no left deviations found. Documented: sponsored SB 214 to defund Planned Parenthood and advanced the Heartbeat Bill as Senate President; drove income- and small-business tax cuts and balanced budgets; voted YES on pro-gun HB 234 (2014, thanked by Buckeye Firearms); expanded school choice; fiscal-watchdog Auditor (150+ public-corruption convictions). Nuance on elections: floated one fraud theory (2022) but AFFIRMED the 2020 result and called Ohio's elections well-run — a moderating, not election-denial, posture (left null). NRA letter grade could not be verified. Confidence evidence_state.

4.note: Sponsored SB 214 to DEFUND Planned Parenthood; endorsed by Ohio Right to Life PAC — clearly not abortion-industry-funded (HARD).

0.note: Expanded school choice as Senate President; as Auditor took a pro-EdChoice posture (surveyed schools over the anti-voucher lawsuit) (HARD/SOFT).

2.note: As Senate President drove income- and small-business tax cuts and prioritized balanced budgets; fiscal-watchdog record as Auditor (opposes deficit spending) (HARD).

1.note: Voted YES on pro-gun HB 234 (2014) expanding firearm rights/reducing restrictions; Buckeye Firearms thanked 'President Faber' (HARD recorded vote).

set.office: Former Attorney General (resigned 2026-06-07 to join Alliance Defending Freedom)

dave-yost.notes_set: CURRENCY: Dave Yost RESIGNED as Ohio AG effective June 7, 2026 to become VP of Strategic Research & Innovation at Alliance Defending Freedom; Gov. DeWine appointed Andy Wilson as the 52nd AG (sworn in June 8, 2026). Yost is NO LONGER AG and is not running for any 2026 office (suspended his 2026 governor bid May 2025 after the OH GOP endorsed Ramaswamy). Status set to former. Substantial AG record: defended the Heartbeat Law and opposed Issue 1; defended HB 68 (trans-care/sports ban) up through the Ohio Supreme Court; filed religious-liberty amici for churches and religious schools; prosecuted noncitizen voting; sued the Biden administration repeatedly (OSHA + CMS vaccine mandates, DHS parole-in-place amnesty, census count of illegal immigrants); pushed to outlaw sanctuary cities. CROSS-PARTY FLAG: in 2020 he did NOT join the GOP-AG effort to overturn Biden's win and opposed Texas's remedy on federalism grounds. Confidence evidence_state.

4.note: Defended the Heartbeat Law / opposed Issue 1; joined the pro-life legal group ADF — clearly not abortion-industry-funded (SOFT).

2.note: Defended HB 68 (ban on minor gender-transition care + Save Women's Sports Act) up through the Ohio Supreme Court: 'no way I'll stop fighting to protect these unprotected children' (HARD).

1.note: Defended HB 68 restricting minor gender-transition interventions (HARD).

0.note: Openly faith-driven; left office to lead research at the Christian legal group ADF, framing it around religious liberty and human dignity (HARD/SOFT).

1.note: Filed amicus briefs defending churches' and religious schools' religious-liberty/conscience rights (HARD).
Prosecuted noncitizen voting — secured indictments of six non-citizens for illegal voting (citizenship-verification enforcement) (HARD).

0.note: Career prosecutor (Delaware County 2003-2011, won the county's first capital case); AG office runs BCI (HARD).

1.note: Sued the Biden OSHA private-employer + CMS healthcare-worker vaccine mandates as 'a dangerous and unlawful use of executive power' (HARD).

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on religion (church/religious-school amici) and federal health mandates (HARD).

3.note: Led/joined multistate suits asserting state sovereignty against federal action (HARD).

4.note: Joined suit to exclude illegal immigrants from census apportionment counts — opposing federal capture of state representation/data (HARD).

2.note: Pushed the legislature to outlaw sanctuary cities; localities 'can't obstruct federal enforcement of federal law' (HARD).

set.office: Attorney General (sitting; 2026 R gubernatorial runoff vs Mazzei, Aug 25)

gentner-drummond.notes_set: CURRENCY: Sitting Oklahoma AG (elected 2022); did NOT seek AG re-election — running for GOVERNOR in 2026. In the June 16, 2026 GOP primary he did NOT win outright (~25% vs Mike Mazzei ~25%); advanced to an Aug 25, 2026 RUNOFF vs Mazzei (whom Trump endorsed). Dem nominee Cyndi Munson awaits the runoff winner. Conservative AG litigation record: defended SB 613 (minor gender-care ban, Poe v. Drummond) and beat the Biden Title IX gender rule; supported proof-of-citizenship voter rules; led the 25-state win against the ATF pistol-brace rule; defended OK's HB 4156 immigration law and anti-sanctuary posture; champions the foreign-(Chinese)-farmland issue (Operation Blunt Force). MAJOR DEVIATIONS (all cited): SUED TO BLOCK the St. Isidore Catholic charter school (against Gov. Stitt & Supt. Walters), winning at the OK Supreme Court — took the church-state-separation side; moved to VACATE Richard Glossip's death sentence (prosecutorial-error/integrity, case-specific); fired the state's anti-ESG lawyer and feuded with the GOP Treasurer amid 'BlackRock ties' allegations. Confidence evidence_state. LIFE cells null: his record is AG-duty defense of existing bans plus a moderating opinion (self-induced abortion not chargeable), no abolition/personhood advocacy.

2.note: Defended SB 613 (felony ban on minor gender-transition care) as named defendant; sued the Biden DOE over the Title IX gender-identity rule and got it blocked (HARD).

0.note: SUED to block the St. Isidore Catholic virtual charter school (a school-choice/religious-education expansion) on establishment-clause/taxpayer grounds, against Gov. Stitt and Supt. Walters; won at the OK Supreme Court (HARD) — documented action against a school-choice option.

1.note: Defended SB 613 restricting minor gender-transition interventions (HARD).
Joined briefs urging SCOTUS to confirm states may require proof of citizenship to vote (RNC v. Mi Familia Vota; 25-state Virginia noncitizen-purge brief) (HARD).

0.note: Operation Blunt Force dismantled a ~$1.5B Chinese-syndicate illegal-marijuana network; 150 pending illegal-grow cases (HARD). NOTE: Glossip vacate-motion noted; cell [4] held null (case-specific, not anti-death-penalty).

1.note: Sued/blocked multiple Biden rules (Title IX, ATF pistol-brace, immigration) (HARD).

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on guns (led 25-state ATF pistol-brace challenge) and education (Title IX) (HARD).

3.note: Defended OK's HB 4156 immigration law asserting state authority against federal preemption challenge (HARD).

2.note: Defended HB 4156 criminalizing illegal presence, framed as mirroring federal law / anti-sanctuary; later backed by Trump DOJ + 22 states (HARD).

4.note: Aggressive enforcement against Chinese-linked illegal farms; champions the foreign-farmland-ownership issue (SOFT/HARD).
Led a 25-state coalition against the ATF pistol-brace rule as unconstitutional overreach; won an 8th Circuit injunction (HARD).
